notes: I tried USE_WINED3D, NO_D3D11 and NO_ESYNC but the game don't start or crash after bioshock 2 logo.
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: To get past "Press Space to Continue" one needs to run the game with -nointro - that is, in game properties, set Launch Options: "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro". For whatever reason, -nointro makes the game accept input after loading screen! Presumably the game has similar texture issues with esync as Bioshock (1), but haven't tried without disabling ESYNC. Seems to run fine, mostly. There are some stuttering / hiccups when transitioning from an area to another; seems the game is loading something. They do not hinder gameplay (as these do not happen mid-action) and might occur even on Windows - have not and can not try! I've got graphical glitches at one point in Atlantic Express after Lamb talks you and you fall into water. Been to the second level now, no other glitches / problems so far.
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: Use the launch option "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro". Disable Esync will fix low level of detail, -nointro will fix keyboard input not be registered. Micro stuttering still happens during level transition.
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
I had difficulty getting this to run and show the menu in a fresh Ubuntu 19.04 install with AMD 2200G CPU & integrated vega graphics.

It turned out that my dxvk installation was incomplete - the symptom was the line "err:   DxvkInstance: Failed to create instance" in the proton logs, and the fix was installing the necessary packages, as described here: https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/wiki/Common-issues#vulkan-errors  . In addition, I use the following launch options: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro

To debug this and discover the issue, I needed to first enable Proton logs by creating a Proton user_settings.py config file: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Proton-FAQ#where-is-the-proton-configuration-file
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: Running with default proton (4.2-3) the game has issues following cutscenes as others have reported, the performance is choppy and the textures are awful. Setting launch options "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro" corrects these issues. In my short playtime the game has been running flawlessly.
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: Use 'PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro' as your launch parameters. For me, the game technically worked without any tweaks, but only low-res textures loaded quickly before disabling esync. In general, textures were too blurry to make out the text and images on posters, and on some objects I could see higher resolution textures progressively loaded in in sequence in front of me. Disabling esync has given me performance I understand to be comparable to the Windows version, meaning occasional stuttering as entering and looking at new areas for the first time.
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: used the same startup commands as bioshock remastered and the game booted and ran perfectly. START OPTIONS: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: To have a near-perfect experience, I needed the following command: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro. E-Sync makes the texture loading really slow and opening a menu in-game takes more than 10 seconds, disabling it stops this behaviour. "-nointro" skips the game intro but allows the game to continue when "press space to continue" is showed, another workaround is to use a game controller instead of the keyboard (at least when this screen is shown).
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: I played the game from start to finish and didn't notice any issues. Just simply set the launch options as 'PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro'.
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: used launch option -nointro (without the game won't start), migrated my save through steamclound from windows and the game worked better under proton, some occasional crashes (far less then on Windows!)
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: Using the same launch options from the original's remaster (PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro), it runs great. Switching workspaces seems to freeze the game, but if you don't do that it works perfectly. Might have crashed once during the ~7.5 hours I played the game for, but I think that's generally because of the instability of this version of the game.
title: BioShock™ 2 Remastered

notes: Use the -nointro flag. Works much better than Bioshock 1, much more stable.
